title: 'I wish this was AI': Parents outraged over abuse of toddlers at Bengaluru daycare
content: Shocking allegations of toddler abuse at a Bengaluru daycare, operating on Capgemini's campus, have ignited outrage among working parents. Demands for livestreamed activities and constant surveillance are surging. The incident highlights a critical lack of oversight, leaving vulnerable children at risk and shattering parental trust in essential childcare support systems. Companies are now urged to implement stringent safety measures and robust governance for workplace daycares.

title: 'We did something nobody knew': Trump details secret US operations near Strait of Hormuz
content: President Trump revealed US forces have repeatedly destroyed Iran's radar systems, forcing Tehran to rebuild them multiple times. He detailed stealth operations along the coast and strikes in response to attacks on shipping.  Meanwhile, indirect talks in Doha concluded without a major breakthrough, focusing on maritime traffic and unfreezing funds, with nuclear program discussions expected later.

title: Born 50 yrs after Kasparov! How Pratitee became India's lone medallist at World Youth Chess
content: Born exactly 50 years after Garry Kasparov, 13-year-old chess prodigy and maths Olympiad star Pratitee Bordoloi scripted history by winning India's only medal at the 2026 FIDE World Youth Chess Championship. The story explores her extraordinary rise, analytical brilliance, disciplined upbringing, and the unique blend of academic excellence, humility, and competitive ambition shaping her journey.

title: Fuel-hit Russia bought gasoline produced by India’s Nayara via traders: Report
content: Russia is reportedly sourcing gasoline from India via international traders to counter fuel shortages stemming from Ukrainian attacks on its infrastructure. Nayara Energy, with a significant stake held by Russian oil giant Rosneft, is identified as a key supplier. While Indian officials state no direct sales, they acknowledge indirect sourcing through trading firms. This development follows reports of substantial gasoline shipments from India to Russia.

title: Microsoft launches FDE division with 6,000 engineers to deliver AI outcomes
content: Microsoft is launching a new venture, Microsoft Frontier Company, backed by a $2.5 billion investment and 6,000 experts. This initiative aims to help businesses achieve tangible results from their AI investments by co-designing, deploying, and continuously improving AI systems. The move addresses customer concerns about rising AI costs and the need for measurable returns, positioning Microsoft to amplify customer intelligence and drive business outcomes.

title: Ayodhya trust not answerable to government, MHA told CIC last year
content: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, managing the Ayodhya temple, operates independently, with the Home Ministry confirming it's not accountable to the government. This autonomy is crucial as the trust faces a July 6 meeting to address resignations amid a donation row. Decisions on key figures like Champat Rai and Anil Mishra will be made internally by its members, highlighting the trust's self-governing structure.

title: Who is the quickest at WC? This Australian tops speed charts ahead of Mbappe, Haaland
content: Australia's Jordan Bos is a breakout star at the FIFA World Cup, showcasing blistering speed that outpaces Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland. The 23-year-old full-back, now a key player for the Socceroos, faces Egypt in a historic knockout clash. Bos's rapid ascent from Australia's domestic league to European giants highlights his impressive talent as the nation chases a World Cup breakthrough.

title: After Meta Layoffs, Zuckerberg says firms should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, to offset significant investments in its AI "reboot." While Meta pours billions into AI infrastructure, Zuckerberg suggests a focus on human empowerment could create more jobs, a view contrasting with some industry leaders.

title: Can a landlord cut your power or water? Know your rights as a tenant
content: The Delhi High Court has reinforced that landlords are not permitted to disconnect essential services like electricity to evict tenants. Recognized as a fundamental necessity linked to the right to life, electricity cannot be withheld even amidst disputes. The Model Tenancy Act safeguards tenants by prohibiting such actions, allowing them to seek recourse through the Rent Authority for swift restoration of services and potential compensation.

title: 'We know he is present with us': Ronaldo, Portugal pay tribute to Jota after win over Croatia
content: Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal paid tribute to late teammate Diogo Jota after a dramatic 2-1 World Cup win over Croatia. Ronaldo wore Jota's No. 21 shirt after the match and scored a historic equaliser, becoming the oldest player to score in a men's World Cup knockout game. Gonçalo Ramos netted the winner as Portugal advanced to face Spain next Monday.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.

title: Vijay' 'Jana Nayagan' release date: CBFC's conditions & OTT release deadline
content: Vijay's upcoming film 'Jana Nayagan' faces a complex release scenario. The CBFC has suggested edits for its 'A' certificate, citing violence and political content, while a character name also needs modification. Meanwhile, distributors and the OTT platform are at odds over the release date, with distributors preferring July 24 and the streaming partner pushing for an earlier July 16 slot to align with an August 15 premiere.

title: ICAI CA foundation May 2026 result at 11am: Check scorecard, passing marks, download steps
content: The Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) will announce the CA Foundation May 2026 result today at 11 am. Candidates can access their scorecards through the official websites using their registration number and roll number. The result will include subject-wise marks, overall status and other key details. Candidates securing at least 40 per cent in each paper and 50 per cent aggregate will qualify for the next stage, the CA Intermediate course.

title: NFL Trade Rumor: 49ers, Cowboys linked to Browns' $4.65M QB
content: The Cleveland Browns’ star player Shedeur Sanders has been a popular name concerning trade rumors ahead of the new season, sparking concern about his future. Amid all the teams that have been linked to him, it seems the Dallas Cowboys and the San Francisco 49ers have emerged as potential landing spots for him.

title: Alex Ovechkin's NHL future in doubt after final-game claim by insider
content: Alex Ovechkin has yet to confirm whether he will return for another NHL season, but recent developments suggest retirement is becoming increasingly likely. TSN insider Darren Dreger believes Washington's offseason moves indicate the Capitals expect their captain not to return. Ovechkin has said family, health and the team's Stanley Cup outlook will guide his decision, while head coach Spencer Carbery and general manager Chris Patrick maintain that no final choice has been made. As the organization waits for an answer, attention remains fixed on what could be the closing chapter of one of the NHL's greatest careers.

title: NHL Trade Rumor: Penguins, Rangers linked to $120M Stars scorer
content: Jason Robertson’s contract negotiations with the Dallas Stars are ongoing, and arbitration is a real possibility. Teams like the Pittsburgh Penguins and New York Rangers appear to be interested in the uncertainty, while Dallas is still looking for ways to keep one of the NHL’s top offensive talents before the 2026-27 season.

title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce 'already married' in secret ceremony?
content: Whispers are swirling that Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce might have already tied the knot in a secret ceremony before their reported July 3rd wedding. Sources suggest the couple exchanged vows privately, possibly in Tennessee, with their private jet making stops near family residences fueling speculation. If true, the upcoming Madison Square Garden event will be a grand celebration of their already-sealed union.

title: Potholes surface on Rs 12,000cr Delhi-Dehradun expressway after season’s first rain
content: A newly inaugurated Rs 12,000-crore Delhi-Dehradun Expressway faced immediate criticism after significant potholes emerged following the first heavy rain in Shamli, Uttar Pradesh. One traveler described the craters as deep enough to damage vehicle alloys and cause near accidents. Authorities quickly deployed repair teams, attributing the damage to intense rainfall. The incident has sparked concerns regarding the expressway's construction quality.

title: Ronaldo's 'last dance'? Sister reveals Portugal legend will retire after World Cup
content: Cristiano Ronaldo's illustrious international career is reportedly nearing its end, with his sister suggesting the 2026 World Cup will be his "last dance" for Portugal.  The football icon recently became the oldest player to score in a World Cup knockout match, a dramatic 2-1 victory over Croatia.  Despite facing criticism, Ronaldo's resilience and continued record-breaking performances underscore his enduring legacy.

title: Salman Khan house firing case: Lawrence Bishnoi's brother seeks surrender
content: Alleged gangster Anmol Bishnoi, wanted in the Salman Khan firing case, has moved a special court in Mumbai to "surrender" virtually. Deported last November and currently in Tihar jail for a separate terror financing case, Bishnoi's lawyers stated his intention to join the ongoing trial. Charges were framed in his absence, and witnesses have testified. The court has sought the prosecution's response.

title: Viral app under scanner after videos claimed it can remotely disable e-rickshaws
content: Delhi's transport department is investigating the Chinese app BAT-BMS following viral claims of it remotely disabling e-rickshaws.  The app reportedly exploits unsecured Bluetooth-enabled Chinese battery management systems, allowing unauthorized users to halt vehicles by switching off their discharge function. Officials are verifying the app's capabilities and the security vulnerabilities of these systems.

title: Tibetan protester sets himself on fire outside UN headquarters in New York City
content: A man, identified as Lobga Rangzen, set himself ablaze outside the UN headquarters in Manhattan, carrying a Tibetan flag in a suspected protest.  Witnesses reported seeing him engulfed in flames before being quickly attended to by emergency services.  He was pronounced dead at a hospital.  Leaflets found at the scene bore slogans like "China Out of Tibet," reflecting a long-standing independence movement.

title: 'Are all citizens being made slaves of the Indian govt?': Bombay HC
content: The Bombay High Court has quashed an externment order against a political party office-bearer, stating that opposing government decisions, even through protests and slogans like 'BJP Govt murdabad', cannot be grounds for forcing someone out of their locality. Justice Madhav Jamdar emphasized citizens' fundamental rights to express opinions and protest, questioning the validity of such actions against individuals for voicing dissent.

title: Cristiano Ronaldo scripts history, becomes oldest World Cup knockout goalscorer
content: Cristiano Ronaldo etched his name in World Cup history, becoming the oldest goalscorer in knockout stages at 41 years and 147 days with a penalty against Croatia. This remarkable feat also places him second overall on the all-time World Cup scoring list.  His appearance also marked him as the oldest outfield player to start a knockout match, underscoring his enduring legacy.

title: 'It was a lack of respect': Jaylen Brown rips Celtics after 76ers trade
content: Jaylen Brown criticized the Boston Celtics for what he called a lack of respect during the process that led to his trade to the Philadelphia 76ers, speaking out on a Twitch livestream one day after the deal was finalized. Boston received Paul George and four draft picks in the trade, ending Brown's 10-year run with the franchise that included a 2023-24 championship. Brown pointed to Celtics president Brad Stevens' communication during the process as a source of frustration while adjusting to his new team.

title: Beijing pushes for China-Pak-style economic corridor with Bangladesh and Myanmar
content: China is pushing for an economic corridor linking Bangladesh and Myanmar, mirroring CPEC's strategic importance. This initiative, discussed during Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman's China visit, aims to boost connectivity, with China developing the Mongla port. Beijing has also expressed openness to other nations joining this "transnational initiative," which has drawn India's attention due to its proximity to the eastern border.

title: FIFA World Cup: Ramos, Ronaldo fire Portugal to 2-1 comeback win over Croatia
content: In a thrilling World Cup showdown, Portugal triumphed over Croatia with a 2-1 scoreline, courtesy of a last-minute header by Goncalo Ramos. Croatia initially led with a goal from Ivan Perišić, but Cristiano Ronaldo leveled the match from the penalty spot, making history as the tournament's oldest scorer. A contentious offside call thwarted Croatia's hopes for a late equalizer, marking a bittersweet end to Luka Modrić's remarkable international journey.

title: No sir: All regular teachers at two Delhi govt schools on SIR duty
content: Educators are questioning why classrooms are bearing the brunt of election duty, with entire teaching staffs at two Delhi government schools deployed for Booth Level Officer tasks. This leaves guest teachers to manage hundreds of students, raising concerns about inevitable academic loss, especially for board exam candidates. While acknowledging election importance, teachers advocate for equitable distribution of duties across schools to prevent educational disruption.

title: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba to skip father Ali Khamenei's funeral: Report
content: Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, will skip his father's funeral due to Israeli threats, according to a representative. This follows a stark warning from Iran's Foreign Minister to the US, citing Israeli remarks about Mojtaba Khamenei being "marked for death." Diplomatic talks in Doha reported positive progress on a Middle East MoU, with further discussions planned post-funeral. Ali Khamenei was killed in an airstrike on February 28.

title: Unbelievable! Ben Stokes retires with a record no cricketer has ever achieved
content: Ben Stokes, the recently retired England all-rounder, leaves behind a truly unique Test record: he's the only player to have batted in all 11 positions and dismissed batsmen from every spot in the order. This remarkable feat underscores his immense versatility throughout a distinguished 15-year career, marked by match-winning performances and iconic victories. Stokes' adaptability across batting and bowling cemented his status as one of England's greatest modern cricketers.

title: India's Tokyo drift: PMs open new chapter in ties
content: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's visit marked a significant advancement in India-Japan relations, with both nations forging new agreements in AI, energy, and defense. A roadmap for economic security was adopted to strengthen supply chains for critical minerals and semiconductors.  Discussions also focused on enhancing bilateral trade through a review of the existing CEPA, underscoring a partnership built on deep mutual trust.

title: ‘I cannot hear or speak, but will assist’: Deaf airport staff’s kindness wins hearts
content: A heartwarming airport encounter has captured the internet's attention. A passenger shared a video of Afjal, an IndiGo employee who is deaf and mute, expertly assisting travelers at baggage check-in. The video highlights Afjal's professionalism and cheerful demeanor, proving that effective communication transcends spoken words. Social media users lauded Afjal's dedication and praised the airline for its inclusive hiring practices, sparking a wider conversation about creating more accessible workplaces.

title: Rahul Dravid 2.0? Samit's batting leaves fans stunned with uncanny resemblance
content: Samit Dravid's batting technique in the Maharaja Trophy drew immediate comparisons to his legendary father, Rahul Dravid, with fans noting the striking similarities. While his batting impressed, Samit is carving his own path as a pace-bowling all-rounder, having excelled in age-group cricket. Meanwhile, younger brother Anvay Dravid is also making strides as a wicketkeeper-batter, recently selected for India's U-19 squad.

title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce secretly married in intimate ceremony: Report
content: Pop sensation Taylor Swift and NFL star Travis Kelce have reportedly exchanged vows in a private ceremony, according to a New York Post report.  Sources suggest the couple, both 36, are now legally married, with a grand celebration for over a thousand guests planned at Madison Square Garden later this week.  This intimate union precedes a larger event, hinting at a significant milestone for the high-profile pair.

title: Yamal joins Pele: Spain's Barca teenagers rewrite World Cup history books in style
content: In a historic moment, Spain witnessed young talents Lamine Yamal and Pau Cubarsí become the first teenage pair to start a World Cup knockout match since the legendary Pelé and Altafini in 1958. Yamal shone brightly, earning the Man of the Match title despite struggling with a hamstring concern, while Cubarsí delivered an impeccable defensive performance.

title: Big fish being shielded in Ram Temple donation case, claim Congress & AAP
content: Congress has urged Prime Minister Modi to order a Supreme Court-monitored probe into alleged irregularities surrounding Ram Temple donations, terming it a "chanda chori mega scandal." Citing the UP government's SIT probe as an "eyewash," the party claims top officials are complicit. AAP leader Arvind Kejriwal echoed these sentiments, alleging powerful individuals involved in land deals and temple construction are being shielded while minor figures are arrested.

title: After WhatsApp, Centre sends notices to Telegram, Signal
content: Following a directive to Meta, the Indian government has now issued notices to Telegram and Signal, demanding explanations on their existing username systems. Authorities are seeking details on how these platforms prevent fraud, impersonation, and scams, giving them three days to respond. The government also questioned Telegram's continued offering of its username feature, citing concerns over potential cybercrime.

title: The rise of Mahjong in Visakhapatnam
content: Once confined to niche circles, Mahjong, the centuries-old tile game is drawing a growing community of players in Visakhapatnam

title: Chandanappally church swaps fireworks for a home and hope
content: Parish heeds the Catholicos’ request to forego fireworks for the church festival in the wake of the Mundakkai fireworks tragedy and redirect the savings towards building homes for deserving families

title: Three more held in UP in Maharashtra TET paper leak case
content: A state-appointed special investigation team (SIT) nabbed the trio, identified as Sonu Singh, Mithun Singh and Kapil Dahiya, officials said

title: AMMA crisis: Actor Shwetha Menon says ‘will continue till next election’
content: In a social media post, she alleges that some 10 to 15 members had attended AMMA general body meeting on Thursday ‘with an agenda’ to oust her panel

title: In Visakhapatnam Central Jail, learning skills for life away from crime
content: The first-of-its-kind digital programme in Andhra Pradesh jails is steering remand prisoners toward meaningful employment after serving prison time. Primarily targeting individuals booked under the NDPS Act, the initiative focuses on rehabilitation and future financial independence of the inmates, explores Harish Gilai

title: Why Bengaluru is India’s deadliest metropolitan city for deaths arising from civic negligence
content: For six consecutive years, Bengaluru has recorded the highest number of deaths due to civic negligence among metropolitan cities. Has much changed on the ground with major administrative overhauls? Chetan B C explores.

title: Boulder collapse near Bengaluru: Half-eaten meals remain where a routine workday at quarry turned fatal
content: Most of the workers employed at the quarry are migrant labourers from Yadgir and Raichur in Karnataka, and from Jharkhand, Bihar and Madhya Pradesh

title: Mapping cancer, testing capacity
content: Telangana government’s decision to make cancer a notifiable disease promises to reveal its true burden in the State. But unless treatment infrastructure expands beyond Hyderabad, identifying more patients may only expose a healthcare system already stretched to its limits, writes Siddharth Kumar Singh

title: Urdu is an Indian language, says Murli Manohar Joshi
content: Senior BJP leader Murli Manohar Joshi hailed the efforts made during the NDA government led by former PM Atal Bihari Vajpayee, to encourage Indian Muslims to read the Quran in Urdu instead of Arabic

title: India, New Zealand ink “historic” free trade agreement, to be implemented later this year
content: The FTA, negotiations for which were announced in March 2025 and concluded in December 2025, is one of the fastest to have been negotiated by India

title: PM Modi to make first official visit to New Zealand next week
content: The two nations signed a free ​trade ‌agreement in April that will eliminate 95% of tariffs ‌on goods from ​New Zealand

title: Madhya Pradesh judge faces mob fury as suo motu case to protect judicial officers sits mute in Supreme Court
content: Supreme Court Advocates on Record Association slams recent threats, targeted social media campaign against MP judge; 2021 case to safeguard judges, courts was last posted for hearing in March 2025

title: Bombay High Court directs State to secure former Judge’s safety
content: The Bench said both Justice Patel, his wife and also daughter, who are in Mumbai, should be adequately protected

title: Assam labourer shows 15 documents but fails citizenship test in High Court
content: The documents Aminul Hoque, a daily-wage labourer, submitted included a 1973 land purchase deed, a school certificate, and a copy of the 1951 National Register of Citizens

title: If a passport is not proof of citizenship, then what is? | Explained
content: The MEA in a statement said that a passport is a travel document, not a citizenship document. What does the law actually say?

title: After WhatsApp, Telegram and Signal receive notices on username feature
content: The notices follow directions to WhatsApp to pause its username feature over scam and impersonation concerns; Spokespersons for Telegram, Signal did not have an immediate comment to share

title: Monsoon tracker July 2, 2026 updates: Man dies after falling into manhole amid heavy rains in Mumbai
content: The incident occurred in the Chandivali area, where waterlogging apparently concealed the uncovered manhole

title: Women Farmers’ Empowerment Bill passed unanimously in Maharashtra Assembly
content: M.S. Swaminathan remembered in the House as Chennai-based MSSRF played a key role in drafting of the Bill

title: Mumbai rains: Man dies after falling into manhole; four BMC officials suspended
content: As the monsoon hit Mumbai from the last week of June, the casualties in Mumbai have become evident with Shaikh’s death being the third incident

title: Kuki village torched in Manipur’s Noney district in pre-dawn attack
content: This follows similar attacks on Kuki and Naga villages in Kamjong district, near the India-Myanmar border, on July 1

title: VB-G RAM G scheme rolled out from Mukkavaripalli village in Andhra Pradesh
content: Launched at Mukkavaripalli in Tirupati district, the renewed rural employment scheme puts farmer welfare first; the Centre also offered to procure 2.16 lakh tonnes of Totapuri mangoes

title: A shot at life, Mandsaur’s model for HPV vaccination
content: This district in Madhya Pradesh has demonstrated effective grassroots strategies for HPV vaccination delivery

title: J&K CM Omar Abdullah backs efforts to resume dialogue with Pakistan
content: Refers to remarks by Vajpayee, and the RSS chief while calling for improved relations with the neighbouring country

title: Wangchuk's health worsens; CJP alleges police assault
content: Dipke reiterates demand for Pradhan's resignation; agitation receives fresh backing from CPI(ML) Liberation General Secretary Dipankar Bhattacharya and RTI activist Nikhil Dey

title: India remains on top in AIU's June list of global dope offenders
content: India has ranked in the top two for the most Anti-Doping Rule Violations (ADRVs) in athletics between 2022 and 2025

title: Transfer of narcotics convict from Sri Lanka to India: Madras High Court penalises MEA & MHA officials
content: Justices Anita Sumanth and Sunder Mohan direct the two joint secretaries in the ministries to pay ₹50,000 to High Court Legal Services Committee

title: SC refuses urgent hearing on plea against Madras HC order quashing appointment of personal assistants to judges
content: The High Court had found several ‘illegalities and irregularities’ in the appointment process undertaken in 2023

title: Privatisation of power distribution: Escoms to submit objections before KERC
content: Escoms have been asked to submit their disagreement and inform the KERC that the government does not concur with the application: CM

title: Karnataka set for rollout of VB-G RAM G scheme
content: Legal decks have been cleared for the rollout of the VB-G RAM G scheme in Karnataka with the State government issuing a notification in this regard on Thursday. The notification comes on the eve of the meeting of RDPR Ministers convened by the Centre in Delhi

title: Two Kashmir Shia clerics ‘disallowed’ to travel to Iran for Ali Khamenei’s final rites
content: J&K Anjuman-e-Sharie Shian president Aga Syed Hassan Al-Mosavi Al-Safavi was stopped by Bureau of Immigration; Shia cleric Masroor Abbas Ansari was also “denied a passport”

title: Debilitating disability: struggles of families of children with chronic disabilities in Kerala
content: Families of children with chronic diseases are struggling to manage their lives and the medical treatment of their wards, finds C. Maya. Experts in the field highlight the need to evolve a societal support system for the families

title: ‘Agri’ Krishnamurthy challenges deserters to prove the alleged bid to form government with DMK’s support
content: All the 47 legislators who had won on the ticket of the AIADMK had adopted a resolution, electing Palaniswami the leader of the legislature party, he says

title: Cauvery realisation by Tamil Nadu in June back to the usual deficit pattern
content: the State realised a little less than three thousand million cubic feet, as against the stipulated 9.19 tmc ft for the month

title: Bandra is disappearing: Is this what Mumbai calls development?
content: The suburb is changing fast. Will redevelopment transform Mumbai into a city of identical towers instead of distinct neighbourhoods?

title: Indian team discovers rare ‘bow and arrow’ radio galaxy two billion light years from Earth
content: The RAD-BAARG may be one of the clearest known radio signatures of a giant bow shock generated by a galaxy falling supersonically into a cluster environment, the researchers say

title: ‘Jana Nayagan’ likely to get CBFC nod after long delay, official says
content: The film’s release, originally slated for January, may finally be released at some point following the issue of a certificate in the coming days

title: Krishnalanka police station staff destroyed evidences in custodial death case: SIT
content: Accused constables did not answer any queries during the questioning, SIT officials tell court; head constable Sambaiah and constable Babu Rao allegedly deleted CCTV footage and other evidence

title: Chennai Corporation fixes September 30 as deadline for road cuts by other service departments
content: The civic body requires all departments to conclude excavations by this date. It will authorise subsequent road cuts only after December. Work to restore major road cuts is already under way

title: Will bring out policy to make getting education loans easier, says P. Viswanathan
content: He says all Collectors have been instructed to organise counselling sessions for students with bank managers in their respective districts to help them acquire loans to pursue higher education

title: No word on hike in minimum pension, allege trade unions
content: The notification does not reflect the demands of subscribers such as increase in minimum pension and clarity in providing higher pension for the applicants, trade unions said

title: Cab vs Car vs Metro: What's The Best Way To Get To Work In Delhi-NCR
content: We spoke to two people who think about this for a living. Their views, laid side by side, map out exactly why this city can't make up its mind.

title: Iran Warns Tankers To Follow Approved Hormuz Routes Or Face Forceful Response
content: Iran threatens forceful response against tankers defying its Hormuz routes, as tensions rise amid US talks and Khamenei's funeral.

title: Secret Service Missed 102 Radio Calls During 2024 Trump Shooting: Report
content: A new report exposes severe security failures at the July 2024 Trump rally, revealing the Secret Service missed 102 critical radio alerts.

title: Nearly 3,000 Evacuated As Wildfires Sweep Across France
content: Nearly 3,000 people evacuated in southern France as wildfires spread near Sainte-Marie-la-Mer and Canet-en-Roussillon after a record heatwave.

title: Ex-Olympian Charged For Causing $1,000 Damage To Trump-Backed Reflecting Pool
content: Former Olympic canoeist David Hearn faces a felony charge for allegedly ripping up sealant at the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington.

title: Appoint Compliance Officials: Centre Seeks Strict Rules For VPN Providers
content: The move follows fresh discussions within the government on strengthening enforcement of the framework amid growing concerns over the misuse of VPN services to conceal identities.

title: How A 100-Hour Mission By 8 Countries Saved Venezuelan Man After Earthquake
content: The back-to-back quakes, measuring magnitudes 7.2 and 7.5, brought down a seven-storey building and damaged tens of thousands of structures across northern Venezuela.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Opens 500 Points Higher Amid Mixed Global Cues
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Oil prices held steady at $72 the tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz increased.

title: Indian Sailor's Organs Missing From Repatriated Body, New Delhi Seeks Answers From Venezuela
content: Chauhan's family has alleged that almost all internal organs of the sailor, including the brain, heart, and lungs, were missing.

title: On Camera, Pune Fort Murder Accused Siya Goyal Shows Middle Finger To Media
content: A 17-second video posted by news agency IANS on X showed Siya walking out of her Market Yard residence in Pune - where the cops had taken her for investigation.

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Moderate Rain To Continue In Mumbai, Delhi Today
content: Strong westerly winds likely to prevail over the southern peninsular India and central India till July 8, the India Meteorological Department said.

title: Choosing A WhatsApp Username? Expert Warns Of 6 Cybersecurity Risks
content: India recorded 24.65 lakh cyber fraud complaints. AI scams, digital arrests, fake investment schemes and identity theft are already rising rapidly.

title: 'Will Surrender Before Court': Jailed Gangster Anmol Bishnoi On Salman Khan House Firing
content: Anmol, the younger brother of jailed gangster Lawrence Bishnoi, was deported from the US in November last year and was subsequently arrested by the National Investigation Agency.

title: Divorce, 2nd Marriages And Wills: Why High-Value Assets Remain Undocumented
content: A robust estate plan goes beyond a will, especially for blended families. It typically includes an updated will and a private family trust.

title: Iran To Hold 6-Day Funeral For Ali Khamenei, Son Mojtaba Won't Attend
content: Ali Khamenei, who ruled Iran for 36 years, was killed on February 28 - the day the US and Israel began striking Iran, triggering a war in the Middle East.

title: "First He Cheats, Then Calls Me": Siya Goyal's Video From Pub Surfaces
content: The barely 15-second clip begins with Siya answering a call and saying "Kaun" (who's this)? She covers one ear with her hand, trying to listen to the caller amid loud background music.

title: 'Don't Tell My Wife I'm Alive Yet...': Venezuelan Man's Plea Before Rescue From Rubble
content: Hernan Alberto Gil Flores emerged to safety covered in dust atop a stretcher surrounded by helmet-clad rescue workers after being trapped since June 24 under rubble

title: IISc study finds ultrasound can selectively destroy oral cancer cells
content: Chethan Kumar is a Senior Assistant Editor with the Times of India. Aside from specialising in Space & Science, he has reported extensively on varied topics, with special focus on defence, policy and data stories. He has covered multiple elections, too. As a young democracy grows out of adolescence, Chethan feels, there are reels of tales emerging which need to be captured. To do this, he alternates between the mundane goings-on of the Common Man and the wonder-filled worlds of scientists and scamsters, politicians and soldiers. In a career spanning nearly 18 years, he has reported from multiple datelines — Houston, Florida, Kochi, Hyderabad, Chennai, Sriharikota (AP), NH-1 (J&K Highway), New Delhi, Ahmedabad, Raichur, Bhatkal, Mysuru, Chamarajanagar, to name a few —  but is based out of Bengaluru, India’s science capital that also hosts the ISRO HQ. Read More

title: PM Modi to make first official visit to New Zealand next week
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most."
